author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the same words of the Gospel of John. xxi. 15, ‘Simon, son of John, lovest thou me more than these?’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the same words of the Gospel of John. xxi. 15, ‘Simon, son of John, lovest thou me more than these?’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye remember that the Apostle Peter, the first of all the Apostles, was disturbed at the Lord’s Passion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of his own self disturbed, but by Christ renewed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he was first a bold presumer, and became afterwards a timid denier.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had promised that he would die for the Lord, when the Lord was first to die for him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When he said then, “I will be with Thee even unto death,” and “I will lay down my life for Thee;” the Lord answered him, “Wilt thou lay down thy life for Me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Verily I say unto thee, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny Me thrice.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They came to the hour; and because that Christ was God, and Peter a man, the Scripture was fulfilled, “I said in my panic, Every man is a liar.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Apostle says, “For God is true, and every man a liar.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ true, Peter a liar.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what now?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord asketh him as ye heard when the Gospel was being read, and saith to him, “Simon, son of John, lovest thou Me more than these?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He answered and said, “Yea Lord Thou knowest that I love Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And again the Lord asked this question, and a third time He asked it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when he asserted in reply his love, He commended to him the flock.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For each several time the Lord Jesus said to Peter, as he said, “I love thee;” “Feed My lambs,” feed My “little sheep.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this one Peter was figured the unity of all pastors, of good pastors, that is, who know that they feed Christ’s sheep for Christ, not for themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was Peter at this time a liar, or did he answer untruly that he loved the Lord?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He made this answer truly; for he made answer of that which he saw in his own heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas when he said, “I will lay down my life for Thee,” he would presume on future strength.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now every man knows it may be what sort of man he is at the time when he is speaking; what he shall be on the morrow, who knows?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then Peter turned back his eyes to his own heart, when he was asked by the Lord, and in confidence made answer of what he saw there: “‘Yea, Lord, Thou knowest that I love Thee.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What I tell Thee, Thou knowest; what I see here in my heart, Thou seest also.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, he did not venture to say what the Lord had asked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Lord had not simply said, “Lovest Thou me?” but had added, “Lovest thou Me more than these?” that is, “Lovest thou Me more than these here do?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was speaking of the other disciples; Peter could not say ought but, “I love Thee;” he did not venture to say, “more than these.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would not be a liar a second time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It were enough for him to bear testimony to his own heart; it was no duty of his to be judge of the heart of others.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter then was true; or rather was Christ true in Peter?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now when the Lord Jesus Christ would, He abandoned Peter, and Peter was found a man; but when it so pleased the Lord Jesus Christ, He filled Peter, and Peter was found true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Rock (Petra) made Peter true, for the Rock was Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what did He announce to him, when he answered a third time that he loved Christ, and a third time the Lord commended His little sheep to Peter?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He announced to him beforehand his suffering.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “When thou wast young,” saith He, “thou girdedst thyself, and wentest whither thou wouldest; but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thine hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Evangelist hath explained to us Christ’s meaning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “This spake He,” saith he, “signifying by what death he should glorify God;” that is that he was crucified for Christ; for this is, “Thou shalt stretch forth thine hands.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where now is that denier?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then after this the Lord Christ said, “Follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not in the same sense as before, when he called the disciples.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For then too He said, “Follow Me;” but then to instruction, now to a crown.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was he not afraid to be put to death when he denied Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was afraid to suffer that which Christ suffered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now he must be afraid no more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he saw Him now Alive in the Flesh, whom he had seen hanging on the Tree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By His Resurrection Christ took away the fear of death; and forasmuch as He had taken away the fear of death, with good reason did He enquire of Peter’s love.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fear had thrice denied, love thrice confessed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The threefoldness of denial, the forsaking of the Truth; the threefoldness of confession, the testimony of love.
